title: BuildContext
objectives: Use it to look up inherited widgets and ancestors

sections.markdown: A BuildContext is a handle to the location of a widget in the tree — in fact it IS the widget's Element. It's how you access Theme, MediaQuery, Navigator, and inherited data relative to that position.

BuildContext is the Element. Theme.of(context) walks up the tree from this element to find the nearest matching InheritedWidget and registers a dependency, so the widget rebuilds when that data changes.

Using a context that's above the widget you need (e.g., calling Scaffold.of(context) in the same build method that creates the Scaffold) — it won't find it. Use a Builder or split into a child widget to get a context below the provider.
Using BuildContext across an async gap without checking mounted — after an await the element may be gone. Guard with `if (!context.mounted) return;`.

flashcards.back: A handle to a widget's location in the tree — it is the widget's Element.
Walks up from the context's element to the nearest Theme InheritedWidget and subscribes to changes.
The context is above the Scaffold; use a Builder/child widget to get a context below it.

quiz.explain: Each Element implements BuildContext, marking its place in the tree.

options: Walking up to the nearest InheritedWidget
quiz.explain: It looks up the ancestor chain and registers a dependency.

quiz.q: Using context after an await requires…
quiz.explain: The element may be unmounted after the async gap.

challenges.text: Read Theme and MediaQuery via context and display values.
Fix a Scaffold.of(context) error using a Builder.
Create an InheritedWidget and read it with a .of(context) helper.
Demonstrate and fix a use-context-after-await bug with context.mounted.

interviewQuestions: Why can the 'wrong' context fail to find a Scaffold/Provider?
How do you safely use context after async?
